domain.Name: Customer
domain.Description: All customer-facing data assets including CRM, marketing, and support systems. Encompasses customer profiles, interactions, and engagement metrics.

domain.Name: Finance
domain.Description: Financial data assets spanning core banking, transactions, reporting, and compliance. Includes general ledger, accounts payable/receivable, and regulatory reporting.

domain.Name: Operations
domain.Description: Internal operational data covering HR, service management, change management, and infrastructure. Supports workforce planning and IT service delivery.

classification.Name: PII
classification.Description: Personally Identifiable Information that can be used to identify an individual. Includes names, email addresses, phone numbers, and government IDs.

classification.Name: PCI
classification.Description: Payment Card Industry data including credit card numbers, CVVs, and cardholder data. Subject to strict encryption and access controls.

classification.Name: PHI
classification.Description: Protected Health Information including medical records, health insurance data, and any individually identifiable health information.

classification.Name: Financial
classification.Description: Financial records subject to Sarbanes-Oxley compliance including transaction records, journal entries, and audit trails.

classification.Name: Internal
classification.Description: Internal business data not intended for public disclosure. Requires standard access controls and appropriate handling.

classification.Name: Public
classification.Description: Information approved for public disclosure. No special handling requirements beyond standard data management practices.

system.Name: Salesforce CRM
system.Description: Primary customer relationship management platform. Manages customer accounts, contacts, sales pipeline, and marketing campaigns. Integrates with Data Warehouse for analytics.

system.Name: Core Banking DB
system.Description: Central banking database storing all transaction records, account balances, and customer financial data. Mission-critical system with 99.99% uptime SLA.

system.Name: Data Warehouse
system.Description: Enterprise data warehouse consolidating data from Salesforce, Core Banking, and HR systems. Powers executive dashboards and regulatory reporting.

system.Name: Kinetic Platform
system.Description: Enterprise service management platform handling incidents, change requests, and service requests. Provides workflow automation and self-service portal.
system.Connection Info: https://kinetic.internal | REST API v1 | Basic Auth

system.Name: HR System
system.Description: Human capital management system managing employee records, payroll, benefits, and organizational structure. Source of truth for workforce data.

dataset.Name: Contacts
dataset.Description: Individual people associated with accounts. Contains personal information, communication preferences, and engagement history.

dataset.Name: Accounts
dataset.Description: Companies and organizations with business relationships. Tracks account hierarchy, billing information, and contract details.

dataset.Name: Opportunities
dataset.Description: Sales pipeline records tracking potential revenue from prospect to close. Includes stage progression, forecasting data, and win/loss analysis.

dataset.Name: Transactions
dataset.Description: Complete record of all banking transactions including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and payments. Immutable audit trail for regulatory compliance.

dataset.Name: Banking Accounts
dataset.Description: Customer banking accounts including checking, savings, and investment accounts. Contains account balances, interest rates, and account status.

dataset.Name: Banking Customers
dataset.Description: Customer master records for banking relationships. Includes KYC data, risk profiles, and relationship history.

dataset.Name: dim_customer
dataset.Description: Slowly changing dimension combining customer data from Salesforce and Core Banking. Provides unified customer view with historical tracking.

dataset.Name: fact_transactions
dataset.Description: Fact table containing enriched transaction data with dimensional references. Supports financial reporting, trend analysis, and anomaly detection.

dataset.Name: dim_product
dataset.Description: Product dimension table containing banking product catalog. Includes product categories, pricing tiers, and feature flags.

dataset.Name: Incidents
dataset.Description: IT service incidents reported through the self-service portal and monitoring integrations. Tracks impact, urgency, resolution, and SLA compliance.

dataset.Name: Change Requests
dataset.Description: IT change management records documenting planned infrastructure and application changes. Includes risk assessment, approval workflow, and post-implementation review.

dataset.Name: Service Requests
dataset.Description: End-user service requests for IT provisioning, access management, and general support. Linked to service catalog items and fulfillment workflows.

dataset.Name: Employees
dataset.Description: Master employee records containing personal information, employment details, and organizational assignments. Source of truth for workforce identity.

dataset.Name: Departments
dataset.Description: Organizational hierarchy and department structure. Defines cost centers, management chains, and functional areas.

dataset.Name: Payroll
dataset.Description: Payroll processing records including salary, deductions, tax withholdings, and payment history. Subject to strict financial and privacy controls.

field.Name: contact_id
field.Description: Unique Salesforce identifier for the contact record
field.Business Definition: A unique 18-character Salesforce ID assigned to each individual contact in the CRM system.

field.Name: first_name
field.Business Definition: The given or first name of the contact person.

field.Name: last_name
field.Business Definition: The family or surname of the contact person.

field.Name: email
field.Description: Primary email address for the contact
field.Business Definition: The primary electronic mail address used for business communications with the contact.

field.Name: phone
field.Business Definition: The primary telephone number for reaching the contact.

field.Name: account_id
field.Description: Reference to the parent account this contact belongs to
field.Business Definition: The Salesforce identifier linking this contact to their parent company or organization account.

field.Name: created_date
field.Description: Timestamp when the contact was created in Salesforce

field.Name: account_id
field.Description: Unique Salesforce identifier for the account record
field.Business Definition: A unique 18-character Salesforce ID assigned to each company or organization account.

field.Name: account_name
field.Description: Legal or registered name of the organization
field.Business Definition: The official registered business name of the customer organization.

field.Name: industry
field.Business Definition: The primary industry vertical in which the customer organization operates.

field.Name: annual_revenue
field.Description: Reported annual revenue of the organization
field.Business Definition: The total annual revenue reported by the customer organization, used for account segmentation and prioritization.

field.Name: account_status
field.Description: Current lifecycle status of the account

field.Name: owner_id
field.Description: Salesforce user ID of the account owner
field.Business Definition: The Salesforce user responsible for managing the customer relationship.

field.Name: opportunity_id
field.Description: Unique Salesforce identifier for the opportunity
field.Business Definition: A unique 18-character Salesforce ID for each sales opportunity.

field.Name: opportunity_name
field.Description: Descriptive name for the sales opportunity
field.Example Values: Acme Corp - Enterprise License, Globex - Platform Upgrade
field.Business Definition: A human-readable name describing the sales opportunity and expected deal.

field.Name: amount
field.Business Definition: The projected monetary value of the sales opportunity, expressed in US dollars.

field.Name: stage
field.Business Definition: The current phase of the sales process for this opportunity.

field.Name: close_date
field.Business Definition: The date by which the opportunity is expected to close or the actual close date if already finalized.

field.Name: opp_account_id
field.Business Definition: The Salesforce account associated with this sales opportunity.

field.Name: txn_id
field.Business Definition: A globally unique identifier assigned to each financial transaction at creation time. Immutable once assigned.

field.Name: account_number
field.Description: Banking account number involved in the transaction
field.Business Definition: The bank account number that is the primary party in this transaction.

field.Name: amount
field.Description: Transaction amount in the specified currency
field.Business Definition: The monetary value of the transaction. Positive for credits, negative for debits.

field.Name: currency
field.Business Definition: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the denomination of the transaction.

field.Name: txn_date
field.Description: Date and time the transaction was processed
field.Business Definition: The exact timestamp when the transaction was processed and recorded in the system.

field.Name: txn_type
field.Business Definition: The classification of the financial transaction by its nature and purpose.

field.Name: txn_status
field.Business Definition: The current processing state of the transaction in the settlement lifecycle.

field.Name: account_number
field.Business Definition: The unique account number assigned to each banking account. Format: NNNN-NNNN-NNNN.

field.Name: account_type
field.Business Definition: The classification of the bank account by product type.

field.Name: balance
field.Business Definition: The current available balance in the account after all settled transactions.

field.Name: customer_id
field.Business Definition: The internal customer identifier linking this account to the customer master record.

field.Name: acct_status
field.Business Definition: The operational status of the bank account.

field.Name: customer_id
field.Business Definition: A sequentially assigned numeric identifier for each banking customer.

field.Name: full_name
field.Business Definition: The full legal name of the banking customer as verified during KYC.

field.Name: email
field.Business Definition: The primary email address on file for the banking customer, used for communications and as a cross-system identifier.

field.Name: kyc_status
field.Business Definition: The current status of the customer's KYC compliance verification.

field.Name: risk_score
field.Description: Calculated customer risk score for AML compliance

field.Name: customer_key
field.Description: Surrogate key for the customer dimension
field.Business Definition: A warehouse-generated surrogate key that uniquely identifies each customer dimension record across source systems.

field.Name: source_system
field.Description: Origin system of the customer record
field.Business Definition: Identifies which upstream source system contributed this customer record to the warehouse.

field.Name: source_id
field.Description: Original identifier from the source system
field.Business Definition: The primary key value from the originating source system, retained for lineage and reconciliation.

field.Name: customer_name
field.Business Definition: The consolidated customer name derived from the golden record matching process.

field.Name: effective_date
field.Description: Date this version of the record became active
field.Business Definition: The date from which this version of the slowly changing dimension record is considered valid.

field.Name: is_current
field.Description: Flag indicating whether this is the active record
field.Business Definition: Indicates whether this dimension record is the currently active version (SCD Type 2).

field.Name: txn_key
field.Description: Surrogate key for the transaction fact
field.Business Definition: A warehouse-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying each transaction fact record.

field.Name: customer_key
field.Business Definition: Reference to the customer dimension identifying the account holder for this transaction.

field.Name: product_key
field.Business Definition: Reference to the product dimension identifying the banking product associated with this transaction.

field.Name: txn_amount
field.Business Definition: The monetary value of the transaction converted to the base reporting currency (USD).

field.Name: txn_date_key
field.Description: Date dimension key for the transaction date
field.Business Definition: Date key in YYYYMMDD format referencing the date dimension for the transaction processing date.

field.Name: source_txn_id
field.Description: Original transaction ID from Core Banking
field.Business Definition: The original transaction identifier from the Core Banking system, preserved for audit trail and lineage.

field.Name: product_key
field.Description: Surrogate key for the product dimension
field.Business Definition: A warehouse-generated surrogate key for each product in the catalog.

field.Name: product_name
field.Description: Display name of the banking product
field.Business Definition: The customer-facing name of the banking product or service.

field.Name: product_category
field.Business Definition: The broad category used to group banking products for reporting and analysis.

field.Name: is_active
field.Description: Whether the product is currently offered

field.Name: incident_number
field.Business Definition: A sequentially assigned identifier for each IT service incident, used for tracking and reference.

field.Name: summary
field.Example Values: VPN connectivity issues, Email server unresponsive
field.Business Definition: A concise human-readable summary of the incident for quick identification and triage.

field.Name: inc_status
field.Business Definition: The current lifecycle state of the incident within the ITIL incident management process.

field.Name: priority
field.Description: Calculated priority based on impact and urgency
field.Business Definition: The priority level derived from the combination of business impact and urgency, determining response SLA.

field.Name: assignee
field.Description: Person or team assigned to resolve the incident
field.Business Definition: The individual technician or support team currently responsible for resolving this incident.

field.Name: created_at
field.Description: Timestamp when the incident was reported

field.Name: change_number
field.Business Definition: A sequentially assigned identifier for each IT change request.

field.Name: change_title
field.Example Values: Upgrade PostgreSQL to v16, Deploy new firewall rules
field.Business Definition: A descriptive title summarizing the nature and scope of the planned IT change.

field.Name: risk_level
field.Description: Assessed risk level of the change
field.Business Definition: The assessed risk level based on potential impact to business operations if the change fails.

field.Name: change_status
field.Business Definition: The current state of the change request in the change management lifecycle.

field.Name: request_number
field.Business Definition: A sequentially assigned identifier for each IT service request.

field.Name: request_type
field.Business Definition: The type of IT service being requested, mapped to the service catalog.

field.Name: requester
field.Business Definition: The end user who initiated the service request.

field.Name: req_status
field.Business Definition: The current state of the service request in the fulfillment workflow.

field.Name: requested_date
field.Description: Date and time the request was submitted

field.Name: employee_id
field.Business Definition: A unique identifier assigned to each employee upon hire. Format: EMP-NNNNNN.

field.Name: full_name
field.Business Definition: The employee's full legal name as recorded in official HR documentation.

field.Name: department
field.Business Definition: The organizational department to which the employee is currently assigned.

field.Name: hire_date
field.Business Definition: The official start date of the employee's current employment.

field.Name: salary
field.Business Definition: The employee's current annual base compensation, excluding bonuses and benefits.

field.Name: manager_id
field.Description: Employee ID of the direct manager
field.Business Definition: Reference to the employee's direct reporting manager in the organizational hierarchy.

field.Name: emp_status
field.Business Definition: The current employment status of the individual.

field.Name: department_id
field.Business Definition: A numeric identifier assigned to each organizational department.

field.Name: department_name
field.Example Values: Data Governance, Finance IT, IT Operations
field.Business Definition: The official name of the organizational department.

field.Name: cost_center
field.Business Definition: The accounting cost center associated with this department for financial reporting.

field.Name: head_count
field.Description: Current number of employees in the department
field.Business Definition: The total number of active employees currently assigned to this department.

field.Name: parent_department
field.Description: Parent department in the org hierarchy

field.Name: payroll_id
field.Business Definition: A sequentially assigned identifier for each payroll processing record.

field.Name: pay_employee_id
field.Description: Reference to the employee receiving payment
field.Business Definition: The employee identifier linking this payroll record to the employee master.

field.Name: pay_period
field.Business Definition: The pay period end date identifying which payroll cycle this payment belongs to.

field.Name: gross_pay
field.Business Definition: The total compensation amount before any deductions, taxes, or withholdings.

field.Name: net_pay
field.Business Definition: The final payment amount after all taxes, benefits, and other deductions have been applied.

glossary-term.Name: Customer
glossary-term.Definition: An individual or organization that has an established business relationship with the company, either through direct engagement, purchases, or contractual agreements. A customer may exist across multiple systems with different identifiers.

glossary-term.Name: Account
glossary-term.Definition: A business entity or organizational unit with which the company has a commercial relationship. In banking context, also refers to a specific financial instrument (checking, savings, etc.) held by a customer.

glossary-term.Name: Transaction
glossary-term.Definition: A discrete financial event that changes the balance of an account, including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and payments. Each transaction is atomic, immutable once settled, and subject to audit requirements.

glossary-term.Name: Employee
glossary-term.Definition: An individual engaged by the company under an employment contract, receiving compensation for their work. Distinguished from contractors and temporary workers by their employment status and benefits eligibility.

glossary-term.Name: Department
glossary-term.Definition: A functional organizational unit within the company hierarchy, typically aligned to a cost center and led by a department head. Departments group employees by function or business area.

glossary-term.Name: Revenue
glossary-term.Definition: Income generated from normal business operations including sales of products and services before any expenses are deducted. Revenue is recognized according to ASC 606 guidelines.

glossary-term.Name: Product
glossary-term.Definition: A banking product or service offered to customers, including deposit accounts, lending instruments, and investment vehicles. Each product has defined features, pricing, and eligibility criteria.

glossary-term.Name: Service
glossary-term.Definition: An IT service or business capability provided to internal or external users, defined in the service catalog with associated SLAs, support processes, and fulfillment workflows.

glossary-term.Name: Incident
glossary-term.Definition: An unplanned interruption to an IT service or reduction in the quality of an IT service. Incidents are managed through the ITIL incident management process with defined priority levels and response SLAs.

glossary-term.Name: Change Request
glossary-term.Definition: A formal proposal for an alteration to the IT environment, including modifications to infrastructure, applications, or services. Changes follow a risk-based approval process.

glossary-term.Name: SLA
glossary-term.Definition: A Service Level Agreement defining the expected level of service between a provider and consumer, including metrics for availability, response time, and resolution time.

glossary-term.Name: Contract
glossary-term.Definition: A legally binding agreement between the company and a customer or vendor, defining terms of service, pricing, duration, and obligations of each party.

glossary-term.Name: Invoice
glossary-term.Definition: A commercial document issued to a customer requesting payment for goods or services rendered. Invoices are linked to contracts and tracked through the accounts receivable process.

glossary-term.Name: Payment
glossary-term.Definition: A transfer of monetary value from one party to another in settlement of an obligation. Payments may be received (from customers) or made (to vendors) and are recorded as financial transactions.

glossary-term.Name: Order
glossary-term.Definition: A customer's request to purchase products or services, containing line items, quantities, pricing, and delivery information. Orders progress through fulfillment until completion.

glossary-term.Name: Lead
glossary-term.Definition: A potential customer who has expressed interest in the company's products or services but has not yet been qualified as an opportunity. Leads are managed through the marketing-to-sales pipeline.

glossary-term.Name: Opportunity
glossary-term.Definition: A qualified sales prospect representing potential revenue. Opportunities track the progression of deals through the sales pipeline from qualification to close.

glossary-term.Name: Contact
glossary-term.Definition: An individual person associated with a customer account. Contacts serve as the primary communication touchpoints and may have specific roles such as decision-maker, technical contact, or billing contact.

glossary-term.Name: Asset
glossary-term.Definition: A tangible or intangible item of value owned or managed by the organization, including IT infrastructure, software licenses, and data assets tracked in the configuration management database.

glossary-term.Name: Compliance
glossary-term.Definition: Adherence to laws, regulations, standards, and internal policies governing data handling, financial reporting, and business operations. Compliance requirements drive data classification and retention policies.

relationship.Name: Contacts → Accounts (Salesforce)
relationship.Description: Each contact belongs to exactly one account. The account_id foreign key in Contacts references the primary key in Accounts.

relationship.Name: Opportunities → Accounts (Salesforce)
relationship.Description: Each opportunity is associated with one account. The opp_account_id foreign key references the account primary key.

relationship.Name: Transactions → Banking Accounts
relationship.Description: Each transaction references the banking account involved. The account_number foreign key links to the Accounts table.

relationship.Name: Banking Accounts → Banking Customers
relationship.Description: Each banking account is owned by a customer. The customer_id foreign key references the Customers primary key.

relationship.Name: fact_transactions → dim_customer
relationship.Description: Each transaction fact references the customer dimension via surrogate key.

relationship.Name: fact_transactions → dim_product
relationship.Description: Each transaction fact optionally references the product dimension via surrogate key.

relationship.Name: Employees → Departments
relationship.Description: Each employee is assigned to a department. The department field references the Departments table.

relationship.Name: Employees → Employees (Manager)
relationship.Description: Self-referencing relationship representing the organizational reporting hierarchy.

relationship.Name: Payroll → Employees
relationship.Description: Each payroll record is linked to the employee receiving compensation.

relationship.Name: SF Contacts ↔ Banking Customers (Email)
relationship.Description: Email address serves as a cross-system identifier linking Salesforce contacts to banking customers. Match confidence is medium due to potential email changes.

relationship.Name: SF Accounts ↔ Banking Customers (Conceptual)
relationship.Description: Conceptual mapping between Salesforce account entities and Banking customer entities. Requires manual matching as no direct key exists.

relationship.Name: Employees ↔ SF Contacts (Name)
relationship.Description: Fuzzy match between employee names and Salesforce contact names. Used for internal customer identification.

relationship.Name: dim_customer ← Salesforce Contacts
relationship.Description: The dim_customer dimension in the warehouse is partially derived from Salesforce contact records via daily ETL.

relationship.Name: dim_customer ← Banking Customers
relationship.Description: The dim_customer dimension is also sourced from Core Banking customer records, merged during the ETL golden record process.

relationship.Name: fact_transactions ← Banking Transactions
relationship.Description: The fact_transactions table is populated from Core Banking transaction records via hourly incremental ETL.

relationship.Name: dim_product ← Banking Accounts (Product Catalog)
relationship.Description: Product dimension is partially derived from distinct product types found in Banking Accounts, supplemented with manual product catalog data.

relationship.Name: Salesforce → Data Warehouse
relationship.Description: Data flows from Salesforce CRM to the Snowflake Data Warehouse via daily Fivetran ETL pipeline. Includes Contacts, Accounts, and Opportunities.

relationship.Name: Core Banking → Data Warehouse
relationship.Description: Data flows from Core Banking DB to the Snowflake Data Warehouse via hourly incremental CDC pipeline. Includes Transactions, Accounts, and Customers.

relationship.Name: HR System → Data Warehouse
relationship.Description: Employee and department data flows from Workday to the Data Warehouse via weekly batch ETL for workforce analytics.

relationship.Name: Customer Domain → Salesforce CRM
relationship.Description: The Customer data domain encompasses the Salesforce CRM system as its primary data source.

relationship.Name: Finance Domain → Core Banking DB
relationship.Description: The Finance data domain encompasses the Core Banking database as a primary data source.

relationship.Name: Finance Domain → Data Warehouse
relationship.Description: The Finance data domain encompasses the Data Warehouse as its analytics and reporting platform.

relationship.Name: Operations Domain → Kinetic Platform
relationship.Description: The Operations data domain encompasses the Kinetic Platform for IT service management.

relationship.Name: Operations Domain → HR System
relationship.Description: The Operations data domain encompasses the HR System for workforce management.

relationship.Name: fact_transactions refs Banking Transactions
relationship.Description: The warehouse fact table stores the source transaction ID for audit trail and data lineage back to Core Banking.

relationship.Name: dim_customer refs SF Contact ID
relationship.Description: The dim_customer dimension preserves the original Salesforce contact_id for lineage when source_system = 'Salesforce'.

relationship.Name: dim_customer refs Banking Customer ID
relationship.Description: The dim_customer dimension preserves the original banking customer_id for lineage when source_system = 'CoreBanking'.

relationship.Name: Data Warehouse depends on Core Banking
relationship.Description: The Data Warehouse depends on Core Banking DB for transaction and account data. Outages in Core Banking directly impact warehouse freshness.

relationship.Name: Data Warehouse depends on Salesforce
relationship.Description: The Data Warehouse depends on Salesforce CRM for customer and sales data. API limits or outages delay warehouse refreshes.

relationship.Name: Payroll refs Departments
relationship.Description: Payroll records include department cost center codes that reference the Departments table for financial allocation and reporting.

quality-rule.Name: Email Format - Contacts
quality-rule.Description: Validates that email addresses in the Contacts dataset conform to standard email format. Applied during CRM data import and scheduled weekly validation.

quality-rule.Name: Not Null - Transaction Amount
quality-rule.Description: Ensures every transaction has a monetary value. Null amounts indicate processing failures and must be investigated immediately.

quality-rule.Name: Range Check - Transaction Amount
quality-rule.Description: Validates that transaction amounts are non-zero and within reasonable bounds. Transactions exceeding $10M require manual review.

quality-rule.Name: Referential Integrity - Contact Account
quality-rule.Description: Ensures every contact's account_id references a valid account record. Orphaned contacts indicate data integration issues.

quality-rule.Name: Uniqueness - Employee ID
quality-rule.Description: Validates that employee_id is unique across all employee records. Duplicates would indicate serious HR system data corruption.

quality-rule.Name: Format Check - Account Number
quality-rule.Description: Validates that banking account numbers follow the standard NNNN-NNNN-NNNN format. Non-conforming records may indicate migration errors.

quality-rule.Name: Completeness - dim_customer Source
quality-rule.Expression: source_system IS NOT NULL AND source_system != ''
quality-rule.Description: Ensures every warehouse customer record has a populated source_system field. Missing source lineage breaks data provenance tracking.

quality-rule.Name: Timeliness - fact_transactions Refresh
quality-rule.Description: Validates that the fact_transactions table has been refreshed within the last 2 hours. Stale data impacts real-time financial reporting.

issue.Title: Duplicate 'Account' definition across systems
issue.Description: The term 'Account' has different meanings in Salesforce CRM (company/organization) versus Core Banking DB (financial instrument). This semantic ambiguity causes confusion in cross-system reporting and data integration.
issue.Evidence: Salesforce Account = company entity (45,000 records). Banking Account = financial instrument (89,000 records). Warehouse dim_customer incorrectly joins on account name.
issue.Recommendation: Establish canonical glossary definitions distinguishing 'Customer Account' (CRM) from 'Financial Account' (Banking). Update warehouse ETL to use correct join keys.

issue.Title: Missing owner assignment on Data Warehouse datasets
issue.Description: Three datasets in the Data Warehouse (dim_customer, fact_transactions, dim_product) are assigned to Priya Sharma as a catch-all, but lack formal business ownership. Technical ownership is insufficient for data governance.
issue.Evidence: All three warehouse datasets list a Data Engineering team member as owner, but no business stakeholder from Finance has accepted ownership for governance purposes.
issue.Recommendation: Assign James Wilson as business owner for fact_transactions and dim_product. Assign Sarah Mitchell and James Wilson as co-owners for dim_customer given its cross-domain nature.

issue.Title: Missing classification on 23 fields across systems
issue.Description: A total of 23 fields across all systems lack a data classification label. Without classification, these fields cannot be properly governed and may be exposed to unauthorized access.
issue.Related System: Salesforce CRM,Core Banking DB,Data Warehouse,Kinetic Platform,HR System
issue.Evidence: Automated scan identified 23 fields with empty Classification values: 5 in Salesforce, 3 in Core Banking, 8 in Data Warehouse, 4 in Kinetic Platform, 3 in HR System.
issue.Recommendation: Run classification review with each system owner. Prioritize fields with PII/PCI indicators in their names or data types. Target completion within 30 days.

issue.Title: Inconsistent customer ID formats between systems
issue.Description: Customer identifiers use incompatible formats across systems: Salesforce uses 18-char alphanumeric IDs, Core Banking uses sequential integers, and Data Warehouse uses surrogate keys. No universal customer identifier exists.
issue.Evidence: Salesforce: contact_id = '003Dn00000A1B2C' (String/18). Banking: customer_id = 100234 (Integer). Warehouse: customer_key = 1 (Integer/surrogate). No cross-reference table exists.
issue.Recommendation: Implement a master data management (MDM) solution or cross-reference table mapping all three ID formats. Use email as interim matching key with confidence scoring.

issue.Title: Missing business definitions for warehouse fields
issue.Description: 12 fields in the Data Warehouse datasets lack formal business definitions. This hinders self-service analytics and increases the risk of misinterpretation by business users.
issue.Evidence: Documentation review found 12 of 17 warehouse fields have empty Business Definition values, including critical fields like effective_date, is_current, and product_category.
issue.Recommendation: Schedule working sessions with Priya Sharma and James Wilson to document business definitions for all warehouse fields. Prioritize customer-facing dimensions.

issue.Title: Email format validation failures in Contacts
issue.Description: The email format quality rule identified 847 records (0.68%) in Salesforce Contacts with invalid email formats, including missing TLDs, special characters, and truncated addresses.
issue.Evidence: Quality rule 'Email Format - Contacts' flagged 847 of 125,000 records. Common patterns: 312 missing TLD, 298 internal test addresses (@test.local), 237 truncated at 255 chars.
issue.Recommendation: Implement Salesforce validation rule to prevent invalid emails at entry. Clean existing records via data steward review. Exclude @test.local from production.
issue.Resolution: Implemented Salesforce validation rule. Cleaned 610 records. Remaining 237 under review with sales team.

issue.Title: Type mismatch: employee_id String vs Integer
issue.Description: The employee_id field is stored as String in the HR System (format: EMP-NNNNNN) but referenced as Integer in some downstream reporting queries, causing join failures and data loss.
issue.Evidence: Warehouse ETL job failure log shows 15 CAST errors per month when employee_id 'EMP-001234' cannot be cast to INTEGER. Affected reports: Workforce Analytics, Payroll Summary.
issue.Recommendation: Standardize on String type for employee_id across all systems. Update warehouse ETL to use VARCHAR column. Add data type validation to integration pipelines.

issue.Title: Missing glossary term mappings for 40+ fields
issue.Description: Over 40 fields across all systems lack a mapping to the enterprise glossary, making it impossible to understand which business concepts they represent or to discover related data across systems.
issue.Related System: Salesforce CRM,Core Banking DB,Data Warehouse,Kinetic Platform,HR System
issue.Evidence: Glossary term coverage analysis: 35 of 83 fields have Glossary Term mappings (42%). Most unmapped fields are technical/audit fields (timestamps, status codes, flags).
issue.Recommendation: Prioritize glossary mapping for business-critical fields. Accept that technical/audit fields may not need glossary terms. Target 70% coverage for business fields.

issue.Title: Stale data in dim_product
issue.Description: The dim_product table in the Data Warehouse has not been refreshed in 18 days, despite a weekly refresh schedule. Three new banking products launched during this period are missing from the dimension.
issue.Evidence: dim_product.last_updated max value is 18 days ago. Three new products (Premium CD, Student Checking, Business Money Market) exist in Core Banking but not in warehouse.
issue.Recommendation: Investigate ETL pipeline failure for dim_product refresh. Implement monitoring alerts for stale dimensions. Manual backfill required for the 3 missing products.

issue.Title: Orphaned relationships after system migration
issue.Description: Following the partial migration from legacy CRM to Salesforce, 12 relationship records reference entities that no longer exist in the source system, creating broken lineage paths.
issue.Evidence: Relationship validation scan found 12 references to 'Legacy CRM' system which was decommissioned. Affected relationships include 8 PK-FK and 4 Derived-From links.
issue.Recommendation: Archive or remove the 12 orphaned relationships. Update remaining relationships to reference Salesforce CRM as the current source. Document migration lineage for audit purposes.

change-log.Details: Created Customer data domain with initial scope covering CRM and marketing systems.
